description: Test suite for agent-browser automation skill

test_cases.prompt: Open https://example.com and take a screenshot of the page.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Browser opens the specified URL and saves a screenshot file.

test_cases.prompt: On https://example.com, click the 'More information' link.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Browser clicks the link identified by its text content.
assertions: navigation occurred (URL changed or page updated)
command 'agent-browser click' executed with correct ref

test_cases.prompt: Fill the search box with 'browser automation' and submit the form.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Input field is populated and form is submitted (via Enter or submit button).
assertions: fill command used with expected text

test_cases.prompt: Extract the main heading (h1) text from https://example.com.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Returns the h1 text content.
assertions: get text command executed on h1 element

test_cases.prompt: Wait until the 'Examples' section is visible on the page.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Browser waits until the specified element appears, then continues.
assertions: wait command used with appropriate selector or text condition

test_cases.prompt: Log into https://example.com/login with username 'user@example.com' and password 'Pass123!'. Verify login by checking for 'Dashboard' or user avatar.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Form is filled and submitted; after navigation, presence of dashboard indicators is confirmed.
assertions: login form inputs located and filled
check for post-login elements (Dashboard, avatar, logout button)

test_cases.prompt: Search for 'laptop' on https://shop.com, sort by price low to high, and extract the names and prices of the first 5 results.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Search executed, sort applied, data extracted into structured format (list of objects).
assertions: data includes both name and price for each

test_cases.prompt: Go through all pages of the product catalog (pagination) and collect every product name.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Iterates through pagination links until the last page and aggregates product names.
assertions: on each page, product names extracted
next button clicked repeatedly until disabled or absent
no duplicate product names in final list
total count of products matches expected

test_cases.prompt: Upload the file '/tmp/resume.pdf' to the application form and submit.
test_cases.expected_behavior: File input is targeted and file uploaded; form submitted; confirmation received.
assertions: upload command executed with provided file path
success message or next step appears

test_cases.prompt: Fill out the contact form (name, email, subject, message), submit, and return the confirmation text.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All fields filled, form submitted, confirmation message captured and returned.
assertions: each required field located and filled with appropriate data

test_cases.prompt: Log in once and save the browser state to 'session.auth'. Then in a separate run, load the state and go directly to the protected dashboard without logging in again.
test_cases.expected_behavior: First run saves state; second run loads state and accesses dashboard directly.
assertions: state save command executed after login
state load command executed before navigation to dashboard

test_cases.prompt: Record a video of the entire login process from start to completion as 'login.webm'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Recording starts, login actions performed, recording stopped and saved.
assertions: record start command executed before actions
record stop command executed after login
output video file exists and non-zero size

test_cases.prompt: On an infinite scroll news feed, scroll down repeatedly until no new articles appear, then extract all article headlines.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Scrolls until content stabilizes, then collects all headlines without duplicates.
assertions: waits after each scroll to allow loading
loop termination condition implemented (no new content)

test_cases.prompt: Fill a multi-field form that includes text inputs, a dropdown, checkboxes, radio buttons, and a textarea. Submit and verify all fields were accepted.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All field types handled correctly; submission successful; server accepts data.

test_cases.prompt: Simulate an e-commerce checkout: add item to cart, go to cart, proceed to shipping, fill address, select shipping method, proceed to payment (do not pay), capture the order summary.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Completes steps up to payment page and extracts order summary details (items, subtotal, shipping).

test_cases.prompt: Extract only the products that are 'In Stock' from a product grid that includes stock status badges. Include product name, price, and stock status in the output.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Parses each product card, filters by stock badge, returns structured data for in-stock items only.
assertions: snapshot or get used to collect all product elements
output contains name, price, and stock status for each in-stock product

test_cases.prompt: Mock the API endpoint '/api/product/123' to return a fixed JSON with price 999.99, then navigate to the product page and verify that the displayed price is the mocked value.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Network route is set to mock; product page loads; price element shows 999.99.
assertions: network route command executed with correct URL and mock JSON

test_cases.prompt: Start two separate sessions: 'admin' logs into admin.example.com, 'user' logs into user.example.com. Then in the admin session, verify that the admin panel shows an elevated privileges badge.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Two isolated sessions run concurrently; admin session displays admin-only content.
assertions: each session navigated to respective domain
admin session contains expected admin element

test_cases.prompt: Try to click a button that may not appear immediately (max 10s wait). If it appears, click it; if not, report that the button was not found.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Waits up to 10 seconds for element; clicks if found; reports timeout otherwise.
assertions: snapshot or is visible used in a retry loop

test_cases.prompt: A page is loading slowly. Use a custom wait condition to proceed only when a specific text 'Ready' appears, with a 30-second timeout.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Waits for text condition; aborts after timeout; reports status.
